shared_claims: Mathematical modeling of dome/firmament geometry over flat disc
claim_overlap.summary: The Journal of Geocentric Cosmology published 'Modeling the Celestial Dome' (May 2024) which shares the general framework of mathematically modeling a dome firmament over a flat disc. The disc radius used (~20,008 km) is extremely close to ECM's 20,015 km. However, no ECM-specific vocabulary (ovoid cavity, aetheric medium, Finsler coordinates, field scaling factor) appears. This is a parallel attempt at mathematical flat-earth dome modeling, not a derivative of ECM. The journal also published articles on GRIN (Gradient Refractive Index) firmament modeling (Jan 2025) and a stereographic flat earth model (Aug 2024), suggesting an active community of mathematical flat-earth modelers, but none appear to reference or derive from the ECM specifically.

github_activity.notable: GitHub API unavailable in this environment (gh CLI not installed). The repo john09289/predictions does not appear in web search results, suggesting very low external visibility — it is not indexed or linked by any third-party site found in search.

notes: First run of social monitor. Comprehensive baseline search across 15 queries covering author identity (john09289, Nicholas Hughes, jesusrules27163822), ECM-specific technical vocabulary (ovoid cavity, aetheric medium, Finsler, field scaling factor, aetheric refraction), specific numerical claims (20015 km, 95.2%, 67 predictions), the dome site URL, our review URL, and platform-specific searches (Reddit, Twitter, GitHub, TikTok, Journal of Geocentric Cosmology). Zero results for author activity on any platform. Zero results for ECM-specific vocabulary outside the dome site itself. Zero mentions of our review anywhere. The ECM appears to have negligible external footprint beyond its GitHub Pages site and the confirmed TikTok account.
